title: Axel Bruns on Fabio Giglietto's ICA 2026 paper
snippet: A blog post by Axel Bruns dated June 8, 2026, mentions Fabio Giglietto's presentation of a paper at the 2026 International Communication Association (ICA) conference in Cape Town. The paper focuses on partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and algorithmic amplification on Facebook.
description: A blog post by Axel Bruns dated June 8, 2026, discusses Fabio Giglietto's paper presented at ICA 2026, focusing on partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and algorithmic amplification on Facebook.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: This article, published on January 9, 2026, reports on a research study from the University of Urbino, authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto, which found that Facebook's algorithm prioritizes anti-European, pro-Russian, and conspiracy extremist content over institutional political visibility.
description: This article reports on a research study by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino, detailing how Facebook's algorithm potentially amplifies extremist and conspiracy content, impacting elections.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: Fabio Giglietto, a professor at the University of Urbino, is cited as the author of a research study published on January 7, 2026, that analyzes the effects of Meta's algorithmic choices on the distribution and visibility of political content on Facebook. The study concludes that the algorithm punishes official political content while rewarding anti-European extremists.
description: The article cites Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino as the author of a research study analyzing Meta's algorithmic impact on political content distribution, finding it favors anti-European extremists over official politics.

title: Più visibilità agli estremisti che ai parlamentari, l'impatto del “filtro” di Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: This news article published on January 7, 2026, details findings from a study authored by Fabio Giglietto, a professor at the University of Urbino. The study, titled 'Meta's Political Content Reduction Policy and Italian Parliamentarians' Facebook Visibility,' examines the impact of Meta's algorithmic filter on political content during elections, suggesting it amplified extremist views at the expense of parliamentarians.
description: A news article published on January 7, 2026, details findings from a study authored by Fabio Giglietto, a professor at the University of Urbino, regarding Meta's algorithmic filter and its impact on political content visibility during elections.
